name: feat-audit-log-subsystem
title: Add property-level audit log subsystem
description: Add property-level entity change tracking via EF Core ChangeTracker for opted-in entities + explicit IAuditLog for non-mutation events. Two packages: Headless.AuditLog.Abstractions (contracts) + Headless.AuditLog.EntityFramework (EF Core implementation). Integrates with existing HeadlessEntityModelProcessor pipeline.
verification_command: dotnet build src/Headless.AuditLog.Abstractions && dotnet build src/Headless.AuditLog.EntityFramework && dotnet build src/Headless.Orm.EntityFramework && dotnet test tests/Headless.AuditLog.Tests.Unit

userStories.title: Add ICorrelationIdProvider to Headless.Core [XS]
userStories.description: Sequential: create ICorrelationIdProvider interface + ActivityCorrelationIdProvider implementation in Headless.Core/Abstractions → register as default singleton in OrmEntityFrameworkSetup.AddHeadlessDbContextServices()
acceptanceCriteria: ICorrelationIdProvider interface exists in Headless.Abstractions namespace with string? CorrelationId property
ActivityCorrelationIdProvider reads from System.Diagnostics.Activity.Current?.Id
Registered as TryAddSingleton in AddHeadlessDbContextServices() following ICurrentTenant/NullCurrentTenant pattern
dotnet build src/Headless.Core succeeds
notes: Follow ICurrentTenant → NullCurrentTenant pattern: interface + default implementation
No new package dependencies — Activity is in System.Diagnostics (BCL)

userStories.title: Create Headless.AuditLog.Abstractions package [S]
userStories.description: Sequential: create .csproj → create all types (marker, attributes, enums, options, DTOs, contracts) → create Setup.cs with DI extension → verify build
acceptanceCriteria: Package builds with only Microsoft.Extensions.Options and Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.Abstractions dependencies (no Headless.Domain or Headless.Core references)

Contains: IAuditTracked, AuditIgnoreAttribute, AuditSensitiveAttribute, SensitiveDataStrategy, AuditChangeType, AuditLogOptions, SensitiveValueContext, IAuditLog, IAuditLogStore, AuditLogEntryData, IAuditChangeCapture
IAuditLogStore.Save/SaveAsync takes (object context, IReadOnlyList<AuditLogEntryData>) — context parameter for DbContext passthrough
AddHeadlessAuditLog() extension uses C# 14 extension block syntax
All public types have XML docs
notes: Namespace: Headless.AuditLog for all types
IAuditChangeCapture.CaptureChanges takes IEnumerable<object> (not EntityEntry) to avoid EF Core dependency
AuditLogEntryData uses string? for UserId/AccountId/TenantId (conversion from primitives happens in EF layer)

userStories.title: Create AuditLogEntry entity and configuration [S]
userStories.description: TDD order: write entity configuration test (verify table name, column types, indexes) → create AuditLogEntry entity + AuditLogEntryConfiguration → create ModelBuilder extension → verify PASS
acceptanceCriteria: AuditLogEntry entity has [AuditIgnore] attribute to prevent recursive capture
Composite PK (CreatedAt, Id) for time-range partitioning readiness
JSON columns (OldValues, NewValues, ChangedFields) use string-based value converters with shared JsonSerializerOptions singleton
ConfigureAuditLog() ModelBuilder extension accepts tableName, schema, and optional jsonColumnType for native JSON override
4 indexes created: tenant+time, tenant+entity+time, tenant+actor+time, correlation
ModelBuilder extension uses traditional 'this' parameter style (not extension block)
notes: Follow Headless.Settings.Storage.EntityFramework pattern for ModelBuilder extensions
Id is long with ValueGeneratedOnAdd
AuditChangeType enum stored as default (int) — no string conversion needed

userStories.title: Implement EfAuditChangeCapture [L]
userStories.description: TDD order: write capture tests for Created/Updated/Deleted entities → implement EfAuditChangeCapture → add AuditIgnore/AuditSensitive handling → add owned entity support → verify all PASS
acceptanceCriteria: Created entity: captures all property values in NewValues, OldValues is null
Updated entity: captures only IsModified properties in ChangedFields with OldValues and NewValues
Deleted entity: captures all property values in OldValues, NewValues is null
Updated with no real changes: no entry produced (empty ChangedFields → skip)
[AuditIgnore] property excluded from all dictionaries
[AuditSensitive] Redact: value is '***', Exclude: property omitted, Transform: SensitiveValueTransformer called
Non-IAuditTracked entity skipped in default mode; included in AuditAllEntities mode unless [AuditIgnore] on class
EntityFilter and PropertyFilter predicates respected
Owned entity inherits auditability from owner, EntityType='Owner.OwnedType', EntityId=owner's PK
Shadow properties (PropertyInfo is null) skipped
Property attribute metadata cached in static ConcurrentDictionary per PropertyInfo
ConcurrencyStamp property excluded by default (framework-managed, not domain data)
SensitiveValueTransformer exceptions caught — fallback to Redact strategy with logged warning
notes: This is the core logic — most complex piece. Cast IEnumerable<object> to EntityEntry internally.
Action format: 'entity.created', 'entity.updated', 'entity.deleted'
Use currentUser.UserId?.ToString() (not .Value) for string conversion
Soft-delete (IDeleteAudit.IsDeleted toggle) produces Modified state — consider mapping to 'entity.soft_deleted'/'entity.restored' actions

userStories.title: Implement EfAuditLogStore and EfAuditLog [S]
userStories.description: TDD order: write store tests (entries added to DbContext) → implement EfAuditLogStore → write explicit log tests → implement EfAuditLog → verify PASS
acceptanceCriteria: EfAuditLogStore casts object context to DbContext, calls Set<AuditLogEntry>().Add() for each entry
EfAuditLogStore does NOT call SaveChanges — entries persist with consumer's next SaveChanges
EfAuditLog checks options.IsEnabled before adding entries
EfAuditLog uses IClock.UtcNow for CreatedAt, currentUser.UserId?.ToString() for actor
EfAuditLog sets ChangeType=null for explicit events, stores data dict in NewValues
notes: EfAuditLog needs DbContext — resolve via TryAddScoped<DbContext>(sp => sp.GetRequiredService<TDbContext>()) in AddHeadlessDbContext, or use IServiceProvider
EfAuditLog also needs ICurrentUser, ICurrentTenant, ICorrelationIdProvider, IClock, IOptions<AuditLogOptions>

userStories.title: Integrate with HeadlessEntityModelProcessor and HeadlessDbContext [M]
userStories.description: Sequential: add AuditEntries to ProcessBeforeSaveReport → add optional IAuditChangeCapture? + ICorrelationIdProvider? to processor constructor → add capture call after foreach loop → add store call in CoreSaveChangesAsync/CoreSaveChanges in BOTH HeadlessDbContext AND HeadlessIdentityDbContext → verify existing tests still pass
acceptanceCriteria: ProcessBeforeSaveReport gains IReadOnlyList<AuditLogEntryData>? AuditEntries property
HeadlessEntityModelProcessor takes IAuditChangeCapture? and ICorrelationIdProvider? as optional constructor params (null when audit not registered)
CaptureChanges called after existing foreach loop in ProcessEntries, using db.ChangeTracker.Entries().Select(e => (object)e)
CoreSaveChangesAsync: after ProcessEntries, before transaction/emitter logic, calls store.SaveAsync(this, entries) if entries exist
CoreSaveChanges: same pattern with synchronous store.Save(this, entries)
BOTH HeadlessDbContext AND HeadlessIdentityDbContext updated with audit store call (HeadlessIdentityDbContext has duplicated SaveChanges pipeline)
Store resolved via this.GetService<IAuditLogStore>() from EF Core's hybrid service provider
Capture wrapped in try-catch — exceptions logged, audit skipped, domain save proceeds
When audit not registered: _auditChangeCapture is null → skip capture, GetService returns null → skip store. Zero overhead.
All existing HeadlessDbContext/Processor tests still pass
notes: Critical: the store call must go AFTER ProcessEntries but BEFORE the transaction/emitter branching logic in CoreSaveChangesAsync
Audit entries are just added to the DbContext (not saved) — they persist with the same base.SaveChanges call regardless of transaction path
ProcessBeforeSaveReport is a record — adding a property with default null is backward-compatible

userStories.title: DI registration and Setup.cs for both packages [S]
userStories.description: Sequential: create Setup.cs for abstractions (AddHeadlessAuditLog) → create Setup.cs for EF package (AddHeadlessAuditLogEntityFramework) → create .csproj files → add to solution → verify build
acceptanceCriteria: AddHeadlessAuditLog registers IOptions<AuditLogOptions> via TryAddSingleton
AddHeadlessAuditLogEntityFramework registers IAuditChangeCapture, IAuditLogStore, IAuditLog as scoped services
Both Setup.cs use C# 14 extension block syntax
.csproj files follow conventions: net10.0, no Version attributes, correct ProjectReferences
Headless.AuditLog.Abstractions.csproj references only Microsoft.Extensions.Options and Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.Abstractions
Headless.AuditLog.EntityFramework.csproj references Headless.AuditLog.Abstractions and Headless.Orm.EntityFramework
Both projects added to solution file
dotnet build succeeds for both packages
notes: Check if Microsoft.Extensions.Options and DI.Abstractions need to be added to Directory.Packages.props — they may already be there as transitive deps

userStories.title: Unit tests for EfAuditChangeCapture [L]
userStories.description: TDD order: tests written alongside US-004 implementation. This story covers the comprehensive test suite: Created/Updated/Deleted entities, AuditIgnore, AuditSensitive (all 3 strategies), AuditAllEntities mode, filters, owned entities, composite PK, no-op updates.
acceptanceCriteria: Test project created: Headless.AuditLog.Tests.Unit with RootNamespace=Tests, OutputType=Exe, xunit.v3.mtp-v2
Uses in-memory or SQLite DbContext for unit testing (no external deps)
Tests use snake_case method names following project convention

All 15+ test cases from spec covered: create, update, delete, AuditIgnore property, AuditSensitive Redact/Exclude/Transform, non-IAuditTracked skip, AuditAllEntities, EntityFilter, PropertyFilter, no-op update skip, composite PK, owned entity, AuditIgnore on class
AuditLogOptions defaults tested separately
dotnet test tests/Headless.AuditLog.Tests.Unit passes
notes: This story runs concurrently with US-004 (TDD cycle)
Use Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Sqlite for in-memory testing (already in Directory.Packages.props)

userStories.title: Integration tests for full round-trip [M]
userStories.description: TDD order: write round-trip tests → verify against real DB (SQLite for CI, optionally PostgreSQL via Testcontainers)
acceptanceCriteria: Test project created: Headless.AuditLog.EntityFramework.Tests.Integration
Full round-trip: create entity → SaveChanges → query AuditLogEntry → verify NewValues
Update entity → verify OldValues/NewValues/ChangedFields
Delete entity → verify OldValues only
Transaction atomicity: SaveChanges fails → no AuditLogEntry rows
IAuditLog.LogAsync explicit event → persisted with next SaveChanges
Owned entity round-trip: update owned property → audit entry with correct EntityType/EntityId
notes: Use SQLite for simplicity — it supports all the needed features
Testcontainers PostgreSQL optional (for jsonb column type verification)

userStories.title: README.md files for both packages [S]
userStories.description: Sequential: write README.md for Headless.AuditLog.Abstractions → write README.md for Headless.AuditLog.EntityFramework → include consumer usage example
acceptanceCriteria: README.md exists in both src/Headless.AuditLog.Abstractions/ and src/Headless.AuditLog.EntityFramework/
Abstractions README documents: IAuditTracked, attributes, options, IAuditLog interface, SensitiveDataStrategy
EntityFramework README documents: registration, ConfigureAuditLog(), consumer entity example, explicit logging example
Consumer usage example matches the one in the original spec (Patient entity with IAuditTracked + AuditSensitive)

findings: HeadlessEntityModelProcessor.ProcessEntries iterates ChangeTracker.Entries() in a foreach loop — audit capture hooks after this loop (src/Headless.Orm.EntityFramework/Contexts/HeadlessEntityModelProcessor.cs:163-196)
ProcessBeforeSaveReport is a sealed record with DistributedEmitters and LocalEmitters — adding AuditEntries property is backward-compatible

CoreSaveChangesAsync has complex transaction handling with execution strategies — audit entries must be added BEFORE the branching logic (src/Headless.Orm.EntityFramework/Contexts/HeadlessDbContext.cs:34-97)
DI uses C# 14 extension blocks for IServiceCollection, traditional 'this' for ModelBuilder extensions
UserId/AccountId are IPrimitive<string> wrappers — use .ToString() not .Value for string conversion
Existing AddHeadlessDbContextServices() registers ICurrentTenant, ICurrentUser, IClock, IGuidGenerator via TryAddSingleton — ICorrelationIdProvider follows same pattern
Test projects use RootNamespace=Tests, OutputType=Exe, xunit.v3.mtp-v2, snake_case methods
No prior audit log patterns exist in docs/solutions/ — greenfield implementation
